title: Trump downplays threat to Iran ceasefire after exchange of attacks
subhed: The US claims that Iranian forces launched the first attacks against American destroyers, while Tehran accuses the US military of violating the truce. Despite this, the US president insists that negotiations are progressing, even as he continues to threaten the Islamic Republic.

Macron makes final attempt at reconciliation with Algeria
subhed: More than one year after being recalled to Paris, France's ambassador, Stéphane Romatet, returned to Algeria on Friday to attend the official commemorations of the May 8, 1945 massacres in Setif.

title: Leo XIV's return to realism and formalism at the Vatican
subhed: One year after his election as head of the Catholic Church, Pope Leo, Francis's successor, stands out from the Argentine pontiff in his approach, yet maintains a through line on substance.

title: Annette Messager's exhibition brings her menagerie to life at the Musée de la Chasse et de la Nature
subhed: The artist has installed her birds, giant snails, monsters and whimsical creatures among the taxidermied animals of the Paris museum. Visits are punctuated with surprises and incongruities.

title: Hungary experiences democratic spring on the eve of Péter Magyar's inauguration
subhed: The defeat of outgoing prime minister Viktor Orban, who is set to hand over the reins of government to Péter Magyar on Saturday, is bringing a wave of change to the country. Oligarchs close to the former regime are now facing a surge of investigations.

subhed: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.

title: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'The United States has lost its moral authority for good'
subhed: The United States is turning its back on democracy and freedom – values the nation once embodied – and is now replacing them with the law of the strongest and a culture of deal-making. Donald Trump has caused irreparable harm to the world's leading power, argues the economist in his column.

title: Moscow's Bauman University, the secretive school training Russian hackers
subhed: According to 2,000 documents obtained by a consortium of international media, including Le Monde, the prestigious Bauman Moscow State Technical University has been discreetly training Russian military intelligence officers, particularly hackers.
